subject: UX & Research
title: IA for Voice Interfaces
description: A micro-lesson that makes IA for Voice Interfaces usable.

hook.text: On a screen, users scan 50 options in seconds. With voice, they hear one option at a time. IA must change completely.

buildup.text: Voice interfaces can't show a menu. They must ask questions to narrow down what the user wants step by step.

discovery.text: Amazon's Alexa uses a flat IA: every skill is accessed directly by name. There are no browsable categories in voice.

twist.text: Users forget skill names. Discovery is the biggest problem in voice IA. You can't browse what you can't see.

climax.text: Voice IA succeeds with short, predictable paths. 'Set a timer' works. 'Find me a good restaurant' is harder.

punchline.text: Voice needs flat, predictable paths. Deep hierarchies are invisible in audio.

quiz.question: Why is information architecture fundamentally different for voice interfaces?
options: Users hear one option at a time and can't scan — so structures must be flat and predictable
Voice interfaces have unlimited bandwidth for information
Users prefer hearing long menus read aloud
